text:
Good night after another incredible day in Paris.

And goodbye Andy Murray.

We leave you with this....

Never even liked tennis anyway.

Donna Vekic of Croatia and China's Zheng Qinwen will now play for Olympic gold.

Vekic defeated Slovakia's Anna Karolina Schmiedlova in straight sets, while Zheng ended Iga Swiatek's 25-match winning streak on the Paris clay.

The final is on Saturday. Poland's Swiatek and Schmiedlova will play for bronze tomorrow.

The IOC have issued a strong statement following the boxing controversy that plagued the Games on Thursday.

Italy's Angela Carini quit her contest with Algeria's Imane Khelif in just 46 seconds on Thursday. Khelif had previously been banned from the world championships after being deemed 'biologically male'.

READ MORE HERE.

It is all over now.

No miraculous escape act tonight as Andy Murray and Dan Evans lose 6-2 6-4 to Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul.

Thank you, Andy.



Team USA duo Tommy Paul and Taylor Fritz are on fire against Andy Murray and Dan Evans.

They are now 4-1 up in the second set, having taken the first 6-2.

Team GB's Ben Proud has made the 50m freestyle final in Paris.

"I have never been in this position, it is very exciting," he told the BBC. "That race felt great, a semi-final always will as it is quite relaxed. We will see how the lanes get drawn."

Novak Djokovic is closing in on the semi-finals at the Roland Garros.

He's taken the first set 6-3 against Stefanos Tsitsipas.



Simone Biles leads the women's gymnastics all-around final after being given 14.566 for her routine on the beam.

In second place is Algeria's Kaylia Nemour, who was born in France and has the support of the home crowd.

Team GB have all but secured a place in the quarter-finals of the women's hockey competition after beating the United States 5-2 in their penultimate match in Pool B.

They now await the result of the ongoing match between Spain and South Africa.

Should South Africa fail to win, then Team GB will progress to the knock-out stages.

FULL TIMEWhat a game! A great performance for the women closes the game out at USA 2 - 5 GBR \ud83c\uddec\ud83c\udde7 \ud83c\uddfa\ud83c\uddf8Fantastic goals from Sophie Hamilton, Tess Howard (2), Hannah French and Sarah Jones#Paris2024#Hockeypic.twitter.com/3iClJf6vj9

A sickener for Joe Clarke.

A time of 89.52 and only enough for fifth place. Italy's Giovanni de Gennaro is celebrating madly as he takes gold. Home favourite Titouan Castryck took silver and Spain's Pau Echaniz bronze.

Clarke has his head in his hands. He looks disconsolate.

Here we go, Joe Clarke must beat 88.2 seconds to take gold from Italy's Giovanni De Gennaro....

Italy's Giovanni de Gennaro has clocked a time of 88.22 seconds to move into the gold medal position.

Joe Clarke is waiting patiently. And no doubt tensely....

The double gold medal dream lives on for Carlos Alcaraz.

He came from 5-2 down in the second set against American Tommy Paul to win his quarter-final 6-3 7-5 (9-7).



Play has been suspended at Le Golf National with Hideki Matsuyama leading after his eight-under 63.

With the threat of thunder and lightning, officials have taken players of the course. Xander Schauffele is six under with a hole to play.

And in sailing, there's been more chaos in the men's skiff final. The first attemot was was abandoned earlier today after a wind shift, and now the re-run has been scuppered due to a lack of wind.

Gold medal hope Joe Clarke has qualified fastest for the men's canoe slalom K1 final.

He'll be back in action at 4.30pm and there's another big chance of Team GB glory.



Algerian boxer Imane Khelif is through to the quarter-finals of the women's 66kg after her opponent quit in 46 seconds.

Khelif is cleared to compete in the women's boxing in Paris, having been disqualified from last year's Women's World Championships for failing to meet eligibility criteria.

Italy's Angela Carini was in tears afterwards, and before Khelif's arm was raised, could be heard on camera saying "it's not right."

Tom Daley has complained about the food hall in the Olympic village, saying it 'gets busy' and urging officials to find a way around the problem.

Offering an insight into what goes on behind the scenes in an interview withGQ,Daley said: "The village itself is actually quite great.

"The only thing is the food hall gets busy. Hopefully they’ll figure that out because as the Games go on it's just going to get busier and busier."

Iga Swiatek will not be playing in the women's singles final after suffering a shock defeat to Zheng Qinwen of China.

The world's top-ranked female playerrefused to shake the umpire's handfollowing her 6-2 7-5 defeat and later walked away from an interview to cry.

Although she still has a shot at the bronze medal, Swiatek had made no secret of her desire to win Olympic gold prior to her surprise exit.

Her conqueror Zheng will face either Donna Vekic or Anna Schmiedlova in the final on Saturday.

There has been a dramatic twist in the Olympic sailing with the men's skiff medal race being abandoned.

It was stopped a few moments ago after a huge shift in the wind direction, presumably because officials decided it would give an unfair advantage to some competitiors.

Team GB duo James Peters and Fynn Sterritt were sixth at the time of the stoppage. We are waiting to find out when the race will get back underway.

BBC commentator Niall Myant said: "I have never seen that in a medal race at the Olympic level.

"I can only imagine it's because the breeze has drifted so dramatically the committee have decided it's unfair so they've abandoned the race. Quite the moment.

That would have been a really hard decision. They obviously felt that it was unworkable, it was pushing boats forward that shouldn't have been there and they've decided to let this one go."



Chinese swimmer Pan Zhanle has been accused of cheating by a rival coach after smashing the world record for the men's 100 metre freestyle.

The 19-year-old broke his own record by almost half a second as he finished an entire body length ahead of Australian rival Kyle Chalmers.

Pan's incredible swim prompted a furious reaction from Brett Hawke, who coaches Chalmers.

He said: "It is not humanly possible to beat that field by a body length. I don't care what you say.

"This is not a race thing, this is not against any one particular person or nation, this is just what I see and what I know. That's not real.

"You don't beat that field. Kyle Chalmers, David Popovici, Jack Alexy. You don't beat those guys by one full body length in the 100 freestyle.

"That's not humanly possible, okay, so don't sell it to me, don't shove it down my throat. It's not real."

Team GB hopeful Sky Brown has dislocated her shoulder just days before the women's park skateboarding event at the Olympics.

It is saidthat Brown suffered the injury on Sunday before travelling to Paris for the Games.

She is being attended to by team doctors and physios and still intends to compete, with her event scheduled to begin at La Concorde on Tuesday.

The 16-year-old is one of the best young skaters in the world and has already won a host of competition medals, including bronze in Tokyo three years ago.

Team GB have won their third rowing medal of the day, this time in the men's four final.

Oli Wilkes, David Ambler, Matt Aldridge and Freddie Davidson take bronze having finished behind New Zealand and winners USA.

Moe Sbihi, a former Olympic champion rower, says on BBC One: "I think the boys are going to be really happy with that bronze.

"We had question marks for the double world champions, how did they get into a position where they are celebrating a bronze medal like that, but it is a fantastic medal.

"Something went wrong in those first 10 strokes. When you get bronze, it depends on your perspective, but I'd struggle to say that's a fun result, but it is a first Olympics for them.

"They will feel this hunger and it will spur them on to get a better colour medal."



Team GB have been forced to settle for silver in the women's four final, with the Netherlands taking gold.

Helen Glover, Esme Booth, Sam Redgrave and Rebecca Shorten gave it everything but came up just short, with the Dutch quartet edging over the line in first place.

Glover was aiming to become the first British woman to win three Olympic rowing golds but will have to accept silver instead.

Mathilda Hodgkins-Byrne and Rebecca Wilde have taken the bronze medal in the women's double sculls rowing final.

The British duo, who only confirmed their place at the Olympics in May, will join runners-up Romania and gold medallists New Zealand on the podium.

Dame Katherine Grainger, speaking on BBC One, says: "I cannot begin to tell you the journey this crew has been on.

"To see them with Olympic medals around their necks in a matter of moments this is unbelievable, This is one of the stories of the British rowing team here."

Team GB athlete Bryony Pitman has been knocked out of the women's individual archery by Li Jiaman of China.

Pitman falls to a 6-0 whitewash with Li moving into the quarter-finals at her expense.

Elsewhere in Paris, Emma Reid is out of the women's 78kg judo after losing to Yoon Hyunji of South Korea.



Team GB rower Lola Anderson had the BBC studio in stitches as she revealed what Princess Anne said to her just moments after winning Olympic gold.

The Princess Royal was on hand to present the medals after the women’s quadruple sculls on Wednesday, with Team GB edging out the Netherlands in a dramatic photo finish.

Speaking to the BBC afterwards, Anderson revealed: "She said that it was a bit too close. She said could we have won by a bit more? Which I probably agree with!"

Here's a look at the events in which Team GB will be hoping for gold today...

10.18am - Rowing, Women's Double Sculls Finals (Team GB in action)

10.50am - Rowing, Women's Four Finals (Team GB in action)

11.06am - Rowing, Men's Four Finals (Team GB in action)

4.30pm - Canoe Slalom, Men's Kayak Single Final (Team GB hopes: Joe Clarke)

5.15pm - Gymnastics, Women's All-Around Final (Team GB hopes: Alice Kinsella and Georgia-Mae Fenton)

7.30pm - Swimming, Women's 200m Butterfly Final (Team GB hopes: Laura Stephens)

Bev Priestman, the Canadian football coach at the centre of a spying scandal, has sensationally claimed that the men's side have a 'whole operation' on it.

She also made the astonishing allegation that ‘all top 10 teams spy’ in a bombshell email that emerged after her team were caught flying a drone over an opposition training session, according to theDaily Mail.

It comes after Canada's appeal against their six-point deduction and Priestman's year-long ban was dismissed by the Court of Arbitration for Sport.

In the email, which was reportedly sent in March, Priestman said: "I know there is a whole operation on the men's side with regards to it.

"On Tuesday, in a meeting when discussing, I asked to propose a alternative solution as for scouting it can be the difference between winning and losing and all top 10 teams do it."



Hello and welcome to Express Sport's rolling coverage of the latest news on day six of the Olympics.

Team GB will be hoping to increase their medal tally over the next few hours, with plenty of opportunities for gold, silver and bronze to be won.

We'll be bringing you the biggest stories from Paris throughout the day, so grab yourself a coffee and stay tuned...



Team GBwill be hoping to add to their gold medal tally on day six of theOlympicswith a jam-packed schedule on the horizon. It could be a big day in the rowing, with Helen Glover competing in the women's four alongside Esme Booth, Samantha Redgrave and Rebecca Shorten.

Andy MurrayandDan Evansare back on court at Roland Garros as they face the American pairing of Tommy Paul and Taylor Fritz in the men's tennis doubles. The golf also gets underway with Tommy Fleetwood and Matt Fitzpatrick representing Team GB, whileRory McIlroywill be in action for Ireland.

Simone Biles will be looking to add to her medal tally in the star-studded individual gymnastics competition after clinching team gold earlier this week. Team GB duo Georgia-Mae Fenton and Alice Kinsella will be hoping to upset the apple cart by claiming the spoils for themselves.

Follow Express Sport's live updates below...

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

title: Olympics news: Cheating scandal blows up after bombshell email as Andy Murray exits | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Archie Griggs, Fraser Watson
description: Express Sport brings you the latest news on day six of the Olympics.
